per_site_substr_hash.note: Per-site numbers are cold (Criterion iter_batched PerIteration = fresh arena each call). Warm per-site cost in full panel = total/23 ≈ 83-94 ns.

cross_language_comparison.note: Rust hashrope exactly matches Python byte-slice performance. The Python 18× slowdown was entirely interpreter overhead.

analysis.key_finding: Python hashrope's 18× disadvantage vs byte-slice was 100% interpreter overhead, not algorithmic. Rust hashrope matches Python byte-slice at 2.1 µs for 23-site panel.
analysis.limitation: At 1680 bp gene scale, byte-slice (38 ns Rust, 2.1 µs Python) is faster than hashrope because the data fits in cache and memcmp is ~1.4 ns for 3 bytes. Hashrope's O(log w) advantage requires genome-scale data where baseline becomes I/O-bound.
analysis.implication_for_paper: Report as honest constant-factor characterization at small scale. Genome-scale crossover (E-G1) is where the algorithmic advantage materializes.
